perturbation.original_text: This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (the “ Agreement ”), effective July 31, 2014 (the “ Effective Date ”), is by and between BioMarin Pharmaceutical Inc., a Delaware corporation, with offices at 105 Digital Drive, Novato, CA 94949 (“ BioMarin ”), and Prosensa Holding N.V., a Netherlands corporation with offices at J.H. Oortweg 21, 2333 CH, Leiden, The Netherlands. (“ Prosensa ”).
perturbation.changed_text: This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (the “ Understanding ”), effective July 31, 2014 (the “ Effective Date ”), is by and between BioMarin Pharmaceutical Inc., a Delaware corporation, with offices at 105 Digital Drive, Novato, CA 94949 (“ BioMarin ”), and Prosensa Holding N.V., a Netherlands corporation with offices at J.H. Oortweg 21, 2333 CH, Leiden, The Netherlands. (“ Prosensa ”).
perturbation.explanation: The change of 'Agreement' to 'Understanding' creates confusion since the rest of the document refers to the legal document as 'Agreement'. This misaligned terminology introduces an in-text contradiction. Specifically, the phrase 'This Understanding' contradicts subsequent mentions of 'Agreement', leading to ambiguity about the document's nature.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of BioMarin and Prosensa may be referred to herein as a “ Party ” or collectively as the “ Parties .” WHEREAS, the Parties, for their mutual benefit, desire to disclose certain confidential information to one another in order to evaluate a potential business or collaborative relationship. (In the capacity of disclosing information, each Party is referred to as the “ Disclosing Party ”, and in the capacity of receiving information, each party is referred to as the “ Receiving Party ”.) NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the premises and covenants contained herein, the Parties agree as follows: 1. Purpose . The Parties have entered into this Agreement to facilitate the transfer of information between them and/or one or more of their Affiliates in order for the Parties to evaluate whether or not to pursue a potential business opportunity or collaboration with respect to the development and commercialization of Prosensa’s products including, but not limited to, Drisapersen (the “ Purpose ”), and solely for that Purpose, the Parties have disclosed or may disclose to each other information that is proprietary and/or confidential to the Disclosing Party which it desires be treated as confidential. For purposes of this Agreement, with respect to a Party, “ Affiliate ” shall mean a company controlled by, under the control of, or in common control with such Party. 2. Confidential Information .
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the document as an 'Agreement', which is used consistently throughout the 'Contradicted Text' section. The change to 'Understanding' introduces a 'Misaligned Terminology', as the contract now refers to itself by two different names, leading to ambiguity. The 'Contradicted Text' refers to 'this Agreement' in multiple places, directly conflicting with the changed text's introduction of the document as 'this Understanding'. This creates a clear in-text contradiction because the document can't be both an 'Agreement' and an 'Understanding' without further clarification, causing uncertainty about which term is legally binding.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall disseminate Confidential Information only to those employees, independent contractors, advisors, or Affiliates, on a “need to know” basis in order for Receiving Party to carry out the Purpose, and Receiving Party warrants that all such employees, independent contractors, advisors, or Affiliates shall be advised of the confidential nature of the information received and that all such employees, independent contractors, advisors, Affiliates shall be bound in writing by obligations no less stringent than the terms set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall disseminate Confidential Information only to those personnel, temporary staff, consultants, or Subsidiaries, on a “need to know” basis in order for Receiving Party to carry out the Purpose, and Receiving Party warrants that all such personnel, temporary staff, consultants, or Subsidiaries shall be advised of the confidential nature of the information received and that all such personnel, temporary staff, consultants, Subsidiaries shall be bound in writing by obligations no less stringent than the terms set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'employees, independent contractors, advisors, or Affiliates' with 'personnel, temporary staff, consultants, or Subsidiaries' introduces ambiguity. Are these terms interchangeable with the definitions of 'Affiliates' given in section 1 and the references through out the text? This change introduces the in-text contradiction regarding who exactly is bound by the confidentiality clauses under the term 'Affiliates', which is defined in section 1, since those terms are being switched.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Purpose . The Parties have entered into this Agreement to facilitate the transfer of information between them and/or one or more of their Affiliates in order for the Parties to evaluate whether or not to pursue a potential business opportunity or collaboration with respect to the development and commercialization of Prosensa’s products including, but not limited to, Drisapersen (the “ Purpose ”), and solely for that Purpose, the Parties have disclosed or may disclose to each other information that is proprietary and/or confidential to the Disclosing Party which it desires be treated as confidential. For purposes of this Agreement, with respect to a Party, “ Affiliate ” shall mean a company controlled by, under the control of, or in common control with such Party. 2. Confidential Information .
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly defines who is bound by confidentiality as 'employees, independent contractors, advisors, or Affiliates'. The changed text replaces this with 'personnel, temporary staff, consultants, or Subsidiaries'. This introduces a contradiction because 'Affiliates' is explicitly defined in the 'Contradicted Text' section as 'a company controlled by, under the control of, or in common control with such Party.' Replacing 'Affiliates' with 'Subsidiaries' introduces ambiguity about the scope of confidentiality obligations, directly contradicting the defined term 'Affiliates' and potentially altering the obligations regarding which related entities are bound. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant or transfer any right to Receiving Party under any patent, copyright or other intellectual property right of Disclosing Party, nor shall this Agreement grant or transfer to the Receiving Party any right in or to the Confidential Information except as expressly set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing in this Understanding is intended to grant or transfer any right to Receiving Party under any patent, copyright or other intellectual property right of Disclosing Party, nor shall this Understanding grant or transfer to the Receiving Party any right in or to the Proprietary Data except as expressly set forth herein.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Data' and 'Agreement' to 'Understanding' creates confusion. While 'Confidential Information' is defined, 'Proprietary Data' is not. This introduces an in-text contradiction. The document still uses the term 'Confidential Information' frequently, so introducing 'Proprietary Data' for the same purpose leads to ambiguity in whether they are the same, and also with the term the document is still being referred to as.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used herein, “ Confidential Information ” shall mean any and all technical and non-technical information previously, presently, or subsequently disclosed or provided by Disclosing Party and/or one or more of its Affiliates to Receiving Party and/or one or more of its Affiliates in written, oral or electronic form. Confidential Information will be deemed to include, without limitation: (a) any technology, inventions, products, chemical compounds and compositions, formulations, molecules, precursors, methods, concepts, ideas, plans, processes, specifications, characteristics, techniques, know-how and assays; clinical information such as raw data, scientific preclinical or clinical data, observations, records, databases, dosing regimes, clinical studies or protocols, posters, presentations and abstracts, product pipelines, timelines and schedules; business information such as development, marketing, sales, pricing and commercialization plans, forecasts, proposals, customer lists, suppliers, consulting relationships, operating, performance and cost structures, and any other non-public information or other trade secrets, whether scientific, clinical or financial in nature, relating directly or indirectly to the business of the Disclosing Party; and (b) any memorandum, analysis, compilation, summary, interpretation, study, report or other document, record or material that is or has been prepared by or for the Receiving Party and that contains, reflects, interprets or is based directly or indirectly upon any information of the type referred to in Section 2(a) above; (c) the existence and terms of this Agreement, and the fact that information of the type referred to in Section 2(a) above has been made available to the Receiving Party; and (d) the fact that discussions or negotiations are or may be taking place with respect to a possible transaction involving the Parties, and the proposed terms of any such transaction. 3. Term .
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Agreement' with 'Understanding' and 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Data'. 'Confidential Information' is explicitly defined in the 'Contradicted Text', while 'Proprietary Data' is not defined anywhere. This creates a clear contradiction because the document then uses a term ('Proprietary Data') that is undefined but appears to relate to a defined term ('Confidential Information'), leading to ambiguity and potential disputes about which information is protected and what terms of the agreement apply. The change also refers to the contract as 'Understanding', which contradicts the original text which refers to it as the 'Agreement'.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party agrees that disclosure of Confidential Information without the express written permission of Disclosing Party will cause Disclosing Party irreparable harm and that any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party will entitle Disclosing Party to injunctive relief, in addition to any other legal and/or equitable remedies available to it.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party agrees that disclosure of Protected Data without the express written permission of Disclosing Party will cause Disclosing Party irreparable harm and that any breach or threatened breach of this Understanding by Receiving Party will entitle Disclosing Party to injunctive relief, in addition to any other legal and/or equitable remedies available to it.
perturbation.explanation: Switching from 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Data' and 'Agreement' to 'Understanding', which are undefined terms, introduces an in-text contradiction. It is unclear if 'Protected Data' is the same as 'Confidential Information', defined in Section 2. It is also unclear if 'Understanding' is a legal document that provides legal remedies. This is an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used herein, “ Confidential Information ” shall mean any and all technical and non-technical information previously, presently, or subsequently disclosed or provided by Disclosing Party and/or one or more of its Affiliates to Receiving Party and/or one or more of its Affiliates in written, oral or electronic form. Confidential Information will be deemed to include, without limitation: (a) any technology, inventions, products, chemical compounds and compositions, formulations, molecules, precursors, methods, concepts, ideas, plans, processes, specifications, characteristics, techniques, know-how and assays; clinical information such as raw data, scientific preclinical or clinical data, observations, records, databases, dosing regimes, clinical studies or protocols, posters, presentations and abstracts, product pipelines, timelines and schedules; business information such as development, marketing, sales, pricing and commercialization plans, forecasts, proposals, customer lists, suppliers, consulting relationships, operating, performance and cost structures, and any other non-public information or other trade secrets, whether scientific, clinical or financial in nature, relating directly or indirectly to the business of the Disclosing Party; and (b) any memorandum, analysis, compilation, summary, interpretation, study, report or other document, record or material that is or has been prepared by or for the Receiving Party and that contains, reflects, interprets or is based directly or indirectly upon any information of the type referred to in Section 2(a) above; (c) the existence and terms of this Agreement, and the fact that information of the type referred to in Section 2(a) above has been made available to the Receiving Party; and (d) the fact that discussions or negotiations are or may be taking place with respect to a possible transaction involving the Parties, and the proposed terms of any such transaction. 3. Term .
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Data' and 'Agreement' to 'Understanding' creates a contradiction with the definition of 'Confidential Information' in the Contradicted Text (Section 2). The original text clearly refers to 'Confidential Information' as defined and 'Agreement' as the legal contract providing remedies. The changed text introduces undefined terms, creating ambiguity and uncertainty, making it unclear if 'Protected Data' is covered by the definition and if 'Understanding' is an enforceable agreement providing the stated remedies, and is therefore a Misaligned Terminology contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is made under and shall be construed according to the laws of the State of California without regard to any conflict of law principles that would provide for the application of the law of another jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: This arrangement is made under and shall be construed according to the laws of the State of Delaware without regard to any conflict of law principles that would provide for the application of the law of another jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Agreement' to 'arrangement' and 'California' to 'Delaware', the legal basis for the contract becomes ambiguous. Throughout the document, the legal document has been referred to as 'Agreement', this is an in-text contradiction. It contradicts the defined jurisdiction clause. If another state's law is applied, there could be a conflict in how confidential information should be protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Governing Law; Jurisdiction . This Agreement is made under and shall be construed according to the laws of the State of California without regard to any conflict of law principles that would provide for the application of the law of another jurisdiction. Any disputes under this Agreement may be brought in the state courts and the Federal courts located in the Northern District of California, and the parties hereby consent to the personal jurisdiction and exclusive venue of these courts. (g) Injunctive Relief .
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Agreement' to 'arrangement' and 'California' to 'Delaware'. The contradicted text explicitly states that 'This Agreement is made under and shall be construed according to the laws of the State of California'. The change introduces an in-text contradiction as the contract's governing law and the term used to identify the document are altered, thus creating a conflict with the original jurisdiction clause. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because the legal basis and identification of the agreement are conflicting.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement commences on the Effective Date and ends on the date one (1) year thereafter. Receiving Party’s obligations to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement and will be binding upon Receiving Party, its heirs, successors, and assigns for a period of five (5) years from expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Contract commences on the Effective Date and ends on the date two (2) years thereafter. Receiving Party’s obligations to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Contract shall survive termination of this Contract and will be binding upon Receiving Party, its heirs, successors, and assigns for a period of three (3) years from expiration or termination of this Contract.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term 'Agreement' to 'Contract' introduces confusion because the document explicitly defines itself as an 'Agreement' elsewhere. Furthermore, the duration has been modified from 1 year to 2 years and the protection period from 5 years to 3 years. These alterations generate an in-text contradiction regarding the duration of the agreement and obligations related to 'Agreement' as it has been defined in the beginning of the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (the “ Agreement ”), effective July 31, 2014 (the “ Effective Date ”), is by and between BioMarin Pharmaceutical Inc., a Delaware corporation, with offices at 105 Digital Drive, Novato, CA 94949 (“ BioMarin ”), and Prosensa Holding N.V., a Netherlands corporation with offices at J.H. Oortweg 21, 2333 CH, Leiden, The Netherlands. (“ Prosensa ”). Each of BioMarin and Prosensa may be referred to herein as a “ Party ” or collectively as the “ Parties .” WHEREAS, the Parties, for their mutual benefit, desire to disclose certain confidential information to one another in order to evaluate a potential business or collaborative relationship. (In the capacity of disclosing information, each Party is referred to as the “ Disclosing Party ”, and in the capacity of receiving information, each party is referred to as the “ Receiving Party ”.) NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the premises and covenants contained herein, the Parties agree as follows: 1. Purpose .
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'Agreement' to 'Contract' while the contradicted text explicitly defines the document as an 'Agreement'. This introduces misaligned terminology, as the document now inconsistently refers to itself as both 'Agreement' and 'Contract' without clarification. This leads to a contradiction, fitting the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'. Furthermore, the change in the term of the contract from one year to two years and the protection period from five years to three years directly contradicts the expectation of the contract's duration related to 'Agreement' as it has been originally defined.
